info.title: Bluesky HTTP Server
info.description:
REST + WebSocket surface of the Bluesky Queue Server. Drives an experiment from
three angles: **queue** (plans and instructions waiting to run), **Run Engine**
(control over the currently-running plan), and **worker environment** (startup
scripts, plans, devices, and tasks inside the worker process).

All endpoints live under `/api`. Clients authenticate with a bearer token
(access or API key); anonymous access, when enabled, is scoped to `read:status`
only. Most endpoints return `{"success": bool, "msg": str, ...}` envelopes —
inspect `success` on each response.

### WebSocket endpoints (not listed below)

Three push streams are available; they do not render in Swagger UI because
OpenAPI has no WebSocket concept:

- `GET /api/status/ws` — pushes the `/api/status` payload as it changes
  (scope `read:monitor`).
- `GET /api/info/ws` — pushes system-info snapshots (scope `read:monitor`).
- `GET /api/console_output/ws` — pushes captured worker stdout/stderr
  (scope `read:console`).

Clients authenticate WebSockets by passing the bearer token via a cookie or
the `Authorization` header on the upgrade request.

get.description: Returns a minimal response from RE Manager — a lightweight way to confirm the server is reachable and the manager process is responsive. Required scope: `read:status`.
Returns a minimal response from RE Manager. Same handler as `/api/ping`. Useful as a basic reachability/liveness check. Required scope: `read:status`.

Returns a status snapshot of RE Manager — manager state, environment state, the currently running item (if any), worker process status, queue/history counts, plus the UIDs clients use for change detection when polling. Required scope: `read:status`.
Returns the manager's client-visible configuration dictionary (the subset of settings considered safe to expose). Required scope: `read:config`.

post.description: When autostart is enabled, the queue starts automatically once the environment is opened, and the manager resumes the queue automatically after a plan pauses. Parameter: `enable` (bool). Required scope: `write:queue:control`.

Configure queue-level execution options such as `loop` (re-run the queue indefinitely) or `ignore_failures` (continue after a failed plan). Parameter: `mode` (dict of option names to values). Required scope: `write:queue:control`.

get.description: Returns the current queue — the list of queued items, the currently running item (if any), the `plan_queue_uid` used for change detection, and the active `running_item_uid`. Required scope: `read:queue`.

post.description: Remove all items from the queue. The currently running plan is not affected. Required scope: `write:queue:edit`.

Begin executing items from the queue. Additional items can be added to the queue while it is running. If the queue is empty, the request succeeds and nothing runs. Required scope: `write:queue:control`.

post.summary: Request queue stop after current plan
post.description: Request the queue to stop after the currently running plan completes. The running plan itself is not interrupted. Rejected if no plan is currently running. Use `/queue/stop/cancel` to back out of a pending stop request. Required scope: `write:queue:control`.
Cancel a previously-issued `/queue/stop` request while the running plan has not yet completed. Always succeeds; a no-op if no stop is pending. Required scope: `write:queue:control`.

post.summary: Add an item to the queue
post.description: Add a single plan, instruction, or function to the queue. Parameter: `item` (dict) describes what to run; `pos` / `before_uid` / `after_uid` optionally control where in the queue the item is inserted. Required scope: `write:queue:edit`.
Execute the supplied item once, outside the queue. The item does not join the queue and does not appear in queue listings. Parameter: `item` (dict). Required scope: `write:execute`.

post.summary: Add a batch of items to the queue
post.description: Add multiple items to the queue in a single request. Parameter: `items` (list of item dicts). The server validates each item; per-item success/failure is returned in the response. Required scope: `write:queue:edit`.

post.summary: Upload a spreadsheet and enqueue the resulting plans
post.description: Multipart upload: a spreadsheet file is processed (either by a user-provided `spreadsheet_to_plan_list` function in a loaded custom module or by the default processor) and the resulting plan list is added to the queue as a batch. Form fields: `spreadsheet` (file), `data_type` (optional str — hint used by custom processors to pick a parsing strategy). Required scope: `write:queue:edit`.
Replace or patch an existing queue item (identified by `item_uid`) with a new specification. Rejected if the item is not in the queue or is currently running. Required scope: `write:queue:edit`.

post.summary: Remove an item from the queue
post.description: Remove a single item from the queue by position (`pos`) or UID (`uid`). Required scope: `write:queue:edit`.

post.summary: Remove a batch of items from the queue
post.description: Remove multiple items from the queue in a single request. Parameter: `uids` (list of item UIDs). Required scope: `write:queue:edit`.

post.summary: Move an item within the queue
post.description: Reposition a queue item. Source selected by `pos` or `uid`; destination by `pos_dest`, `before_uid`, or `after_uid`. Required scope: `write:queue:edit`.

post.summary: Move a batch of items within the queue
post.description: Reposition multiple items in the queue in a single request. Parameter: `uids` (list of item UIDs) plus a destination selector (`pos_dest`, `before_uid`, or `after_uid`). Required scope: `write:queue:edit`.

get.description: Returns details for a single queue item by position (`pos`) or UID (`uid`). Required scope: `read:queue`.
Returns the list of completed plans in chronological order, plus the `plan_history_uid` for change detection. Required scope: `read:history`.

post.description: Remove all entries from the plan-history buffer. Required scope: `write:history:edit`.
Spawn the RE Worker subprocess and initialize the Run Engine. Required before the queue can execute plans or before scripts/functions can be uploaded. Required scope: `write:manager:control`.

Orderly shutdown of the RE Worker. Rejected if a plan is currently running — call `/queue/stop` or `/re/stop` first, or use `/environment/destroy` for a forceful shutdown. Required scope: `write:manager:control`.

Kill the RE Worker process without waiting for the running plan to complete. Last-resort recovery path — intended for expert operators when the worker is hung and cannot be stopped cleanly. Required scope: `write:manager:control`.

Refresh manager-side caches of plans, devices, and namespace metadata from the running worker. Call after uploading a script that adds or redefines plans/devices so subsequent `/plans/*` and `/devices/*` responses reflect the change. Required scope: `write:queue:control`.
Pause the currently running plan. Parameter: `option` — `'deferred'` (pause at the next checkpoint, safe) or `'immediate'` (pause at the next safe point). Required scope: `write:plan:control`.
Resume execution of the currently paused plan. Required scope: `write:plan:control`.
Stop the currently paused plan. The plan is marked as successfully completed from the Run Engine's perspective. Required scope: `write:plan:control`.
Abort the currently paused plan. The plan is marked as failed, but Run Engine cleanup handlers still run (devices are returned to safe states). Required scope: `write:plan:control`.
Halt the currently paused plan immediately without running cleanup handlers. More aggressive than `/re/abort` — use when cleanup itself is misbehaving. Required scope: `write:plan:control`.

post.summary: List runs produced by the current plan
post.description: Returns runs opened during the currently running plan. Parameter: `option` selects `'active'` (all), `'open'`, or `'closed'`; default `'active'`. See `/re/runs/active`, `/re/runs/open`, `/re/runs/closed` for convenience aliases. Required scope: `read:monitor`.

get.summary: List all runs produced by the current plan
get.description: Convenience alias for `POST /re/runs` with `option='active'`. Returns runs opened during the currently running plan (both open and closed). Required scope: `read:monitor`.

get.summary: List open runs produced by the current plan
get.description: Convenience alias for `POST /re/runs` with `option='open'`. Returns the subset of active runs that have been opened but not yet closed. Required scope: `read:monitor`.

get.summary: List closed runs produced by the current plan
get.description: Convenience alias for `POST /re/runs` with `option='closed'`. Returns runs from the current plan that have been closed. Required scope: `read:monitor`.

get.summary: Get metadata of the currently running plan
get.description: Returns the metadata of the plan currently executing in the Run Engine (run-specific kwargs, scan_id, etc.). Required scope: `read:monitor`.

get.summary: List plans allowed for the current user
get.description: Returns plans the current user's resource group is permitted to execute. Parameter: `reduced` (bool, default `False`) — when `True`, plan descriptions are simplified to save bandwidth. Required scope: `read:resources`.

get.summary: List devices allowed for the current user
get.description: Returns devices the current user's resource group is permitted to use. Required scope: `read:resources`.

get.summary: List all plans registered in the worker
get.description: Returns all plans registered in the worker namespace, not filtered by user permissions. Parameter: `reduced` (bool, default `False`) — when `True`, plan descriptions are simplified to save bandwidth.

get.summary: List all devices registered in the worker
get.description: Returns all devices registered in the worker namespace, not filtered by user permissions. Required scope: `read:resources`.

post.description: Reload allowed-plans, allowed-devices, and user-group-permissions definitions from the paths configured on the manager. Use after editing the underlying files on disk. Required scope: `write:config`.

get.description: Returns the current user-group permissions dictionary. Required scope: `read:config`.

post.description: Replace the current user-group permissions. Parameter: `user_group_permissions` (dict). Required scope: `write:permissions`.

post.summary: Execute a function in the worker
post.description: Execute a function defined in the worker's startup scripts. Parameter: `item` (function-item spec with `name`, `args`, `kwargs`). Returns a `task_uid` — poll `/task/status` and `/task/result` for progress and output. Required scope: `write:execute`.

post.summary: Upload and execute a Python script in the worker
post.description: Send a Python source string to the worker for execution. Parameter: `script` (str). Side-effects (new plans, new devices, redefined functions) are visible in subsequent calls after an `/environment/update`. Returns a `task_uid`. Required scope: `write:scripts`.

get.summary: Get status of one or more worker tasks
get.description: Returns the status of tasks started via `/function/execute` or `/script/upload`. Parameter: `task_uid` (str for a single task, or list of str for multiple). Required scope: `read:monitor`.

get.summary: Get result of a worker task
get.description: Returns the result (or error) of a completed task, or the in-progress status if still running. Parameter: `task_uid` (str). Required scope: `read:monitor`.

post.description: Send a keyboard-interrupt to the IPython-kernel-based worker. No-op for worker configurations that do not use an IPython kernel. Required scope: `write:queue:control`.

Acquire an exclusive lock on RE Manager, preventing other users from altering locked resources. Parameters: `lock_key` (str, required to unlock later), `note` (str, description shown to other users), `scope` (list of `'environment'` and/or `'queue'`). Required scope: `write:lock`.
Release a previously-acquired manager lock. Parameter: `lock_key` (must match the value used at lock time). Required scope: `write:lock`.

get.description: Returns the current lock state: who holds the lock, when it was acquired, the associated note, and which scopes are locked. Required scope: `read:lock`.

post.description: Stop RE Manager. Unlike crash-and-restart behaviour, the manager will NOT be auto-restarted by the watchdog after a stop issued via this endpoint. Required scope: `write:manager:stop`.

post.summary: Kill the manager event loop (testing only)
post.description: Halt the manager event loop to test client-side timeout handling and watchdog restart behaviour. Not for production use. Required scope: `write:testing`.

get.description: Sleep for `time` seconds then return success. Does not block the event loop or manager calls. Used to exercise client timeout handling. Required scope: `read:testing`.
Returns a text/event-stream of captured worker stdout/stderr. The connection stays open and the client reads lines as they arrive. Required scope: `read:console`.
Returns the most recent lines of captured worker console output as a text blob. Parameter: `nlines` (int, default 200). Required scope: `read:console`.

Returns the UID of the current console-output buffer. Pair with `/console_output` to detect when the buffer has been reset (for example after the environment is restarted). Required scope: `read:console`.

get.summary: Fetch new console messages since last UID
get.description: Returns console-output messages accumulated since the `last_msg_uid` supplied by the caller. Initialize with `'ALL'` to receive all buffered messages; on each subsequent call, pass back the UID from the previous response. If the UID is not found in the buffer (rollover), an empty message list and a fresh UID are returned. Required scope: `read:console`.
Return a list of all Principals (users and services) known to the server. Admin-only. Required scope: `admin:read:principals`.
Return information about a single Principal (user or service). Admin-only. Required scope: `admin:read:principals`.

post.summary: Generate an API key for a Principal (admin)
post.description: Mint an API key on behalf of the given Principal. Admin-only; most users should call `POST /api/auth/apikey` instead to mint one for themselves. Required scope: `admin:apikeys`.
Exchange a valid refresh token for a fresh access token and refresh token. Used by long-running clients to keep a session alive without re-authenticating.

delete.description: Mark the given session as revoked so it can no longer be refreshed. A user may revoke their own sessions; revoking another user's session is not currently supported.

post.summary: Generate an API key for the current user
post.description: Mint a new API key for the currently-authenticated user or service. The returned secret is shown only once. Required scope: `user:apikeys`.

get.summary: Get info about the current request's API key
get.description: Return metadata (UUID, scopes, expiration) for the API key used to authenticate this request. Useful for looking up the API key identifier given the secret.

delete.summary: Revoke an API key of the current user
delete.description: Revoke an API key belonging to the currently-authenticated user or service, identified by its `first_eight` characters. Required scope: `user:apikeys`.

get.description: Return information about the currently-authenticated user or service, or `null` for anonymous/public requests.

get.summary: Get the current Principal's roles and scopes
get.description: Return the roles and scopes resolved for the currently-authenticated user or service. Clients use this to decide which UI actions to expose.

post.description: Clear the API-key and CSRF cookies set by browser-based flows. Token-based clients can simply discard their credentials.

HistoryItem.title: HistoryItem
HistoryItem.description:
A history item: a queue item plus the execution ``result`` block.

``result`` is kept free-form in this first cut (tightening it to a typed model is a
noted follow-up).

ItemResponse.title: ItemResponse
ItemResponse.description: Single-item mutations: item_add, item_execute, item_update, item_remove, item_move.

ItemsBatchResponse.title: ItemsBatchResponse
ItemsBatchResponse.description:
Batch mutations: item_add_batch, item_remove_batch, item_move_batch.

``results`` (per-item ``{success, msg}``) is only populated by item_add_batch.

LockInfo.title: LockInfo
LockInfo.description:
The structured lock-info block (``_format_lock_info``). All fields optional so the
empty ``{}`` returned on the failure path validates as an all-``None`` instance.

QueueItem.title: QueueItem
QueueItem.description:
A queue or history item.

``item_uid`` / ``item_type`` / ``name`` are present on a populated item but every field
is optional so that an empty ``{}`` (returned by some handlers on failure/idle) validates.
``args`` / ``kwargs`` / ``meta`` are user-supplied and ``properties`` is added internally;
they are kept free-form to avoid drift on user-arbitrary content.

RunsResponse.title: RunsResponse
RunsResponse.description: re_runs (and its active/open/closed views). ``run_list`` elements are free-form run dicts.

Session.title: Session
Session.description:
This related to refresh tokens, which have a session uuid ("sid") claim.

When the client attempts to use a refresh token, we first check
here to ensure that the "session", which is associated with a chain
of refresh tokens that came from a single authentication, are still valid.

StatusResponse.title: StatusResponse
StatusResponse.description: RE Manager status snapshot (``manager.py`` ``_status_update``). All keys always present.

SuccessMsgResponse.title: SuccessMsgResponse
SuccessMsgResponse.description: Bare ``{success, msg}`` envelope shared by control routes that return no payload.

TaskStatusResponse.title: TaskStatusResponse
TaskStatusResponse.description:
``task_uid``/``status`` are polymorphic: a single str when one UID was queried, or a
list/dict keyed by UID when several were queried.

TaskUidResponse.title: TaskUidResponse
TaskUidResponse.description: Routes that return a task handle: environment_update, script_upload.

APIKeyAuthorizationHeader.description: Prefix value with 'Apikey ' as in, 'Apikey SECRET'
APIKeyAuthorizationHeader.name: Authorization

tags.name: Status
tags.description: Liveness and the manager status snapshot used for polling.

tags.name: Config
tags.description: Client-visible subset of the manager's configuration.

tags.name: Queue
tags.description: Queue-level control: mode, autostart, start, stop, clear.

tags.name: Queue Items
tags.description: Add, remove, move, update, and execute individual queue items.

tags.name: Environment
tags.description: Lifecycle of the RE Worker subprocess that hosts the Run Engine.

tags.name: Run Engine
tags.description: Control the currently-running plan: pause, resume, stop, abort, halt.

tags.name: Runs
tags.description: Bluesky runs produced by the currently-running plan.

tags.name: Plans
tags.description: Plans registered in the worker namespace, filtered by permissions.

tags.name: Devices
tags.description: Devices registered in the worker namespace, filtered by permissions.

tags.name: Scripts & Functions
tags.description: Run user code in the worker: upload scripts, execute named functions, poll task status.

tags.name: Lock
tags.description: Exclusive lock on the manager to prevent concurrent state changes.

tags.name: Manager
tags.description: Lifecycle control of the manager process itself.

tags.name: Testing
tags.description: Endpoints that exist solely to exercise client-side error paths.

tags.name: Console Output
tags.description: Access captured worker stdout/stderr (poll, stream, or buffer UID).

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Session, API-key, and identity endpoints. Each configured authentication provider also exposes provider-specific routes under `/api/auth/provider/{provider}/`.
